segments.text: After this the Lord appointed seventy-two others and sent them two by two ahead of him to every town and place where he was about to go.
He told them,
The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest field.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Go! I am sending you out like lambs among wolves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Do not take a purse or bag or sandals; and do not greet anyone on the road.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When you enter a house, first say, 'Peace to this house.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If someone who promotes peace is there, your peace will rest on them; if not, it will return to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Stay there, eating and drinking whatever they give you, for the worker deserves his wages. Do not move around from house to house.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When you enter a town and are welcomed, eat what is offered to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Heal the sick who are there and tell them, 'The kingdom of God has come near to you.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But when you enter a town and are not welcomed, go into its streets and say,
Even the dust of your town we wipe from our feet as a warning to you. Yet be sure of this: The kingdom of God has come near.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you, it will be more bearable on that day for Sodom than for that town.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment than for you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted to the heavens? No, you will go down to Hades.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whoever listens to you listens to me; whoever rejects you rejects me; but whoever rejects me rejects him who sent me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The seventy-two returned with joy and said,
Lord, even the demons submit to us in your name.
segments.speaker: Seventy-two disciples

segments.text: He replied,
I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I have given you authority to trample on snakes and scorpions and to overcome all the power of the enemy; nothing will harm you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: However, do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: At that time Jesus, full of joy through the Holy Spirit, said,

I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this is what you were pleased to do.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All things have been committed to me by my Father. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, and no one knows who the Father is except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then he turned to his disciples and said privately,
Blessed are the eyes that see what you see.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For I tell you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: The Parable of the Good Samaritan

segments.text: On one occasion an expert in the law stood up to test Jesus.
Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?
segments.speaker: Expert in the law

segments.text: What is written in the Law? How do you read it?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: 'Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength and with all your mind'; and, 'Love your neighbor as yourself.'
segments.speaker: Expert in the law

segments.text: Jesus replied,
You have answered correctly. Do this and you will live.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But he wanted to justify himself, so he asked Jesus,
And who is my neighbor?
segments.speaker: Expert in the law

segments.text: In reply Jesus said:
A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, when he was attacked by robbers. They stripped him of his clothes, beat him and went away, leaving him half dead.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: A priest happened to be going down the same road, and when he saw the man, he passed by on the other side.
So too, a Levite, when he came to the place and saw him, passed by on the other side.
But a Samaritan, as he traveled, came where the man was; and when he saw him, he took pity on him.
He went to him and bandaged his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. Then he put the man on his own donkey, brought him to an inn and took care of him.
The next day he took out two denarii and gave them to the innkeeper.
Look after him, and when I return, I will reimburse you for any extra expense you may have.
segments.speaker: Samaritan

segments.text: Which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man who fell into the hands of robbers?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The expert in the law replied,
The one who had mercy on him.
segments.speaker: Expert in the law

segments.text: Jesus told him,
Go and do likewise.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: At the Home of Martha and Mary

segments.text: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, he came to a village where a woman named Martha opened her home to him.
She had a sister called Mary, who sat at the Lord's feet listening to what he said.
But Martha was distracted by all the preparations that had to be made. She came to him and asked,
Lord, don't you care that my sister has left me to do the work by myself? Tell her to help me!
segments.speaker: Martha

segments.text: The Lord answered,
Martha, Martha, you are worried and upset about many things,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: but few things are needed—or indeed only one. Mary has chosen what is better, and it will not be taken away from her.
segments.speaker: Jesus
